lesson.menu_desc: Polite Requests: Could you / Would you mind + gerund -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Asking for Help -- Polite Requests
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Aline Sberci | Aula 14 -- Asking for Help

listenings.text: Hi Aline, this is Tom. I have a small favor to ask. My workload is really heavy this morning, and I am very busy. Could you cover the ten o'clock client call for me? It is a short call, just a quick check-in. And would you mind double-checking the budget file before I send it? I want to be sure the numbers are correct. If you have a spare minute, that would help me a lot. Thank you so much. I will grab you a coffee, and I will help you with your tasks later this week. Let me know if you can. Talk soon.

This is a quick note to myself before the busy morning starts. First, my battery is low, so I need to borrow a charger from Tom. Then I have to ask him a favor: would he mind covering my afternoon meeting, because my workload is heavy today. After that, I want to ask Maria to double-check the report before the team meeting. I know everyone is busy, so I will be polite and use could you and would you mind. To thank them, I will grab them a coffee and help with their tasks later. I feel ready to ask for help.

extra_audio.text: First, Aline notices that her battery is low, so she asks Tom to lend her a charger. Then she explains that her workload is heavy this morning. After that, she asks Tom to cover her afternoon meeting. Next, she asks him to double-check the budget file before he sends it. Finally, she thanks Tom and offers to grab him a coffee.
